rationale: The citing paper’s stated managerial contribution is explicitly about customer lifetime value (CLV) and churn simulations, while the cited paper (“Valuing Customers”) is directly about customer-based valuation and the impact of retention on value. Given this strong construct-level overlap (CLV/retention/firm value), the citation could plausibly serve as a foundational source rather than a purely incidental mention, even though the raw match only provides DOI-level evidence.
